channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: Iran's crypto tanker tolls are the latest step in its sanctions‑busting trade network
item.description: The Iranian regime has been increasingly using cryptocurrency over the last few years to facilitate cross border oil trading, according to data from Chainalysis.

item.title: Everyone’s awaiting U.S. inflation figures, but bitcoin traders couldn’t care less
item.description: There’s a wide gap between how experts are viewing Friday’s inflation data and how the bitcoin market is pricing in the impending figures.

item.title: Bithumb launches legal action to recover 7 Bitcoin from payout error
item.description: South Korean crypto exchange Bithumb files for a court-approved asset freeze to reclaim 7 BTC remaining from a February payout error.

item.title: CFTC presses case that sports betting is finance, seeks to block Arizona enforcement
item.description: Federal lawsuit argues event contracts are swaps under U.S. law, not state-level gaming law, deepening a split with states that view the products as illegal gambling

item.title: Yuga Labs settles lawsuit against artists accused of copying its NFTs
item.description: Yuga Labs first filed a lawsuit in June 2022, accusing Ripps and Cahen of copying its Bored Ape Yacht Club cartoon ape images and selling lookalike NFTs.

item.title: Canary Capital submits application for US-based spot PEPE ETF
item.description: Canary Capital is preparing to launch an ETF tracking Pepe’s price, despite the token trading 85% below its December 2024 all-time high.

item.title: Anthropic loses early appeal over Pentagon ‘supply chain risk’ label
item.description: “In our view, the equitable balance here cuts in favor of the government,” said a panel of judges from the District of Columbia Court of Appeals.

item.title: 99.99% of Polymarket traders shouldn't quit their day jobs, data suggests
item.description: While nearly 16% of Polymarket users are in profit, only a tiny fraction have made gains large and consistent enough to entertain walking away from their day job.

item.title: XRP slips 4% as selling pressure builds despite ETF inflows
item.description: Elevated volume and modest institutional buying fail to support price, with sellers continuing to dominate near resistance.

item.title: Bitcoin's next big move hinges on oil, and right now it's a total coin flip
item.description: Analysts say a sustained 15%–16% decline in crude could revive Fed rate cut bets, sending BTC higher.

item.title: Chainalysis claims stablecoin volumes could reach $1.5 quadrillion by 2035
item.description: Two macro catalysts — wealth transfer and payment disruption — could push stablecoin usage far beyond baseline growth forecasts, analysts say.

item.title: Fed minutes open door to further rate cuts amid Iran war
item.description: Some officials cautiously eyed a year-end rate cut, but others warned that upward adjustments might be needed if inflation remains above target levels.

item.title: Visa unveils commerce platform enabling autonomous AI shopping
item.description: Visa is rolling out a platform that helps merchants make their product inventories discoverable and purchasable by AI agents.

item.title: Bitcoin under $71,000, ETH, SOL, XRP drop as Iran ceasefire frays within 48 hours of being signed
item.description: Tehran says three clauses of the ceasefire have been breached, oil is rebounding toward $97, and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed despite the deal.

item.title: Bitcoin gets its first working prototype of quantum-resistant wallet rescue tool
item.description: Lightning Labs CTO Olaoluwa Osuntokun demos a prototype that could prevent millions of wallets from being frozen under a future quantum-defense upgrade

item.title: Counterhacker exposes DPRK unit that made $1M a month working IT jobs
item.description: The North Korean IT workers coordinated crypto payments through a server using a shared, easy-to-guess password “123456.”

item.title: Bitcoin tops $72K as $280M in shorts liquidated: Will ‘fragile truce’ hold?
item.description: Bitcoin and global markets rallied after the US and Iran announced a ceasefire, but data shows BTC bears have not closed most of their positions yet.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Bitcoin Depot ATM Operator Says $3.6 Million in BTC Stolen in Corporate Hack
item.description: The Bitcoin ATM operator disclosed a hack two weeks after attackers gained control of settlement account credentials and stole BTC.

item.title: Researchers Propose New Way to Manage Financial Risk When AI Agents Fumble Trades
item.description: A newly proposed agentic settlement standard would hold fees in escrow and bring underwriters into AI agent transactions.

item.title: US SEC taps new enforcement chief amid questions over predecessor’s exit
item.description: David Woodcock steps into the role as US senators await answers to questions on the agency dropping lawsuits against Justin Sun and several crypto companies.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s bitcoin ETF draws $34 million on day one
item.description: Morgan Stanley’s low-fee bitcoin ETF debuted with strong early trading, signaling demand as competition shifts to cost and distribution.

item.title: Bitcoin Miner Cango Sells $143 Million in BTC, Slashes Production Costs
item.description: NYSE-listed miner Cango reduced costs by 19% by shutting down inefficient equipment, and sold Bitcoin to pay down debt.

item.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark, Its Most Capable AI Yet—But Gemini 3.1 Pro Still Leads the Pack
item.description: Meta's first model from its Superintelligence team is natively multimodal, built for health reasoning, and genuinely competitive—but it doesn't top every leaderboard.

item.title: Treasury Outlines How Stablecoin Rules Will Fight Illicit Finance Under GENIUS Act
item.description: The proposed rule prohibits people with criminal backgrounds from serving as the head of stablecoin issuers’ compliance programs.

item.title: Anthropic's Mythos Safety Report Shows It Can No Longer Fully Measure What It Built
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos is powerful, but Its own safety report reveals a deeper crisis that's gone largely unnoticed.

item.title: Bitcoin has 3–5 years to prepare for quantum risk, says Bernstein
item.description: Bitcoin faces a future quantum threat, but Bernstein analysts say risks are concentrated in older wallets and exposed keys, and unlikely to cause existential disruption.

item.title: US Treasury proposes GENIUS Act rules targeting illicit finance
item.description: The proposed rule would direct payment stablecoin issuers to establish AML/CFT and sanctions compliance programs, and be able to “block, freeze, and reject” certain transactions.

item.title: Stablecoin Trading Volume Could Skyrocket to $1.5 Quadrillion by 2035: Chainalysis
item.description: Generational wealth transfer and point-of-sale adoption could drive stablecoin volumes to eclipse traditional payment rails, says Chainalysis.

item.title: Price predictions 4/8: BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, HYPE, ADA, BCH, LINK
item.description: Bitcoin’s relief rally is facing selling pressure near $72,000, but technical charts suggest a bullish bias. Will altcoins follow in BTC’s footsteps?

item.title: Bitcoin Pioneer Adam Back, Bernstein Say Quantum Threat to BTC Isn’t Existential
item.description: Anxieties over the quantum threat to Bitcoin have been growing, but Bernstein backs Back in saying there’s no cause for alarm.

item.title: OpenAI Publishes Child Safety Blueprint to Address AI-Enabled Exploitation
item.description: OpenAI's new policy blueprint outlines steps the industry can take to combat AI-enabled child sexual exploitation.

item.title: Michael Saylor says bitcoin has likely bottomed, quantum risk overblown
item.description: Speaking at a Mizuho event, the Strategy (MSTR) executive chairman said the formation of banking credit pairing with digital credit will be the catalyst for the next bull market.

item.title: Hyperliquid Traders Face Widespread Liquidations as Oil Prices Collapse
item.description: Brent crude oil prices were on track for their biggest daily decline since the start of the COVID-19 pandemic in early 2020.

item.title: Cloudflare Targets 2029 for Quantum-Safe Internet as Threat to Bitcoin Looms
item.description: New research and industry timelines are accelerating efforts to replace cryptography that quantum computers could eventually break.

item.title: Bitcoin eyes $90K as Binance data shows surge in aggressive buying
item.description: Bitcoin gained momentum as data showed buyers are starting to dominate volumes on Binance, with a $90,000 BTC price target on their radar.

item.title: Bitcoin demand returns, giving bulls fuel to turn $72K to support
item.description: Bitcoin buy-side activity across spot and derivatives markets is strengthening, while reduced selling from short-term holders improves the odds of holding $72,000.

item.title: Potential Insiders Made $600K Predicting US and Iran Ceasefire on Polymarket: Bubblemaps
item.description: A trio of accounts on Polymarket made more than $600,000 on U.S./Iran ceasefire markets, drawing attention as potential insiders.

item.title: Yuga Labs settles Bored Ape NFT lawsuit, ending fight over alleged copycat tokens
item.description: The settlement avoids a trial and ends the dispute over the RR/BAYC NFTs, which claimed to parody Bored Ape Yacht Club, one of the most recognizable NFT brands.

item.title: South Korea proposes cryptocurrency law with bank-style rules for stablecoins
item.description: Draft bill outlines comprehensive framework for digital assets, including licensing, issuance and oversight

item.title: Partner of Trump-Backed World Liberty Linked to Sanctioned Cambodian Scam Company: Report
item.description: AB network’s planned “blockchain theme resort” involved individuals sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury over ties to the Prince Group.

item.title: The Protocol: Bernstein says quantum threat to Bitcoin is real but manageable
item.description: Also: North Korea’s 6-month plot with Drift, Solana Foundation’s new ad and Alchemy AI.

item.title: Adam Back Denies He's Satoshi Nakamoto After NYT Names Him as Bitcoin Creator
item.description: John Carreyrou isn’t the first person to link Adam Back and Satoshi Nakamoto based on their writing styles.

item.title: Crypto Long & Short: Asia’s digital asset crackdown: accountability gets personal
item.description: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Bob Williams covers how stricter crypto regulations in Asia are putting more personal responsibility on senior leaders, making strong governance and D&O insurance essential. Then, the FBI’s Ha…

item.title: U.S. Treasury proposes demands that stablecoin firms be set to police bad transactions
item.description: The U.S. is pitching new rules for stablecoin issuers to treat them like every other financial firm that must maintain armor against illicit uses.

item.title: Iran weighing crypto tolls for ships using Strait of Hormuz: Report
item.description: Empty tankers will reportedly be permitted to pass through the waterway under the US-Iran deal, but certain ships will need to pay a tariff of $1 per barrel of oil in Bitcoin.

item.title: Bitcoin is above $70,000 on Iran ceasefire, but rally is turning cautious for good reasons
item.description: Leveraged bullish bitcoin positions remain near multi-year highs as bitcoin rebounds, hinting at underlying market uncertainty.

item.title: Moo Deng Meme Coin Rises After Man Fined for Entering Viral Pygmy Hippo's Den
item.description: The Solana meme coin based on the viral pygmy hippo Moo Deng gained after a man was fined for entering the animal's enclosure.

item.title: SEC Says Prior Crypto Enforcement Set 'Misguided Expectations' As Actions Drop 22%
item.description: Enforcement actions and penalties both fell sharply as the agency moves toward fraud-only crypto oversight.

item.title: Standard Chartered is looking to take over crypto custody provider Zodia: Bloomberg
item.description: While the British financial institution refused to comment on potential takeover plans, sources close to the matter revealed that plans are in place and could be announced as soon as this month.

item.title: Bitcoin fades three-week highs as BTC price shrugs off Iran war ceasefire
item.description: Bitcoin briefly reclaimed $72,000 on ceasefire news, but fading momentum, resistance and macro risks leave traders questioning the strength of the breakout.

item.title: RWA network Pharos lands a $1 billion valuation in $44M funding round ahead of mainnet debut
item.description: The company is building an "asset-native" network designed to handle regulated financial activity at scale, targeting a market it values at $50 trillion.

item.title: Crypto markets are recovering:: Crypto funds recorded +$224 million in inflows last week, posting the 4th weekly intake over the last 5. This was led by XRP, at +$120 million, the largest weekly inflow since December 2025, bringing year-to-date inflows to +$15 - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZkFVX3lxTE5SMmxPYXViUlBnbGpKQ2laTGhvV3NaUXJrQTVROVhXcDh3UGJfcHYtbzlYeDhUUVJzNE9vMVo3c0J4cUF4c2hIdnYtTDhwdFRFWDZqOEVmcFIwRWlmeThZMkRvbXJLQQ?oc=5" target="_blank">Crypto markets are recoverin…

item.title: Iran Wants Bitcoin Payments From Oil Ships Seeking Hormuz Passage: FT
item.description: Iranian officials said Bitcoin payments would ensure the tolls “can’t be traced or confiscated due to sanctions.”

item.title: MEXC targets EU MiCA compliance as new CEO takes helm
item.description: MEXC appointed Vugar Usi as CEO and outlined plans to expand zero-fee trading and pursue MiCA licensing amid growing industry competition.

item.title: Adam Back denies he’s Satoshi Nakamoto after NYT report claims he’s Bitcoin’s creator
item.description: Similarities reflect shared early research, not proof, said Back. Others have questions too.

item.title: White House study bolsters crypto's stance in stablecoin yield fight against bankers
item.description: White House economists said banning rewards wouldn't significantly boost banks' financial health, amplifying the crypto industry view in the Clarity Act debate.

item.title: JUST IN: Morgan Stanley's spot Bitcoin ETF is officially trading on the NYSE 🇺🇸 - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZ0FVX3lxTFBzcy1udjk5Q2kzUW5XQkFKYTNmcmNrdGMwT0ZpVTRMNDZRYWQyYnJfc1Fkb0l4RWgwa2l3c0xRMXFHRE9DWF9rY2V4RGwybHFpNXlOa2NUVXVJNjB5Unp3RkI1ZV9JWEU?oc=5" target="_blank">JUST IN: Morgan Stanley's s…

item.title: 1/ Today, The Ethereum Foundation will convert 5000 ETH to stablecoins via @CoWSwap's TWAP feature as a part of our ongoing work to fund R&D, grants and donations. - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiY0FVX3lxTFBxdXN0R1hpS0VEUHlLeVNHTnBkNWFYelNLc3ZRTVFKYkt0Q2tKZlNEZ01hdXZqSGpwUHJwdzcwdmZCOFlhRUxZVnVfTmtSeEd5R1BSQmtVY0hEd1Via1BpMFJMYw?oc=5" target="_blank">1/ Today, The Ethereum Foundatio…

item.title: Bitcoin Surged Overnight After Iran Ceasefire—This New 'AfterDark' ETF Would've Caught the Move
item.description: Investors gained access to an ETF that’s designed to avoid the U.S. trading session, offering exposure to Bitcoin while Wall Street sleeps.
